approval_notes: Owner-approved F004 plan. Slice 1 (safe fetch guard + SourceAdapter contract in apps/api/app/ingest/) is merged to main (PR #14). This contract governs Slice 2 ONLY.

Standing autonomy boundaries: may branch/implement/test/commit/push/open ONE PR; may NOT merge, flip any passes flag, add a runtime/npm dependency, start Slice 3, or touch the offer_version immutability trigger.
F004 stays passes:false after this slice. A fresh-context Level 2 slice evaluation reviews Slice 2 before Slice 3 proceeds.
objective: Add DB persistence and scan orchestration to the ingestion pipeline. Migration 0004 introduces a pre-publication `candidate` table and a `discovery_candidate` community-quarantine table, and relaxes `evidence` so candidate-stage evidence can link Source+Snapshot+Candidate without an offer_version (offer_version_id becomes nullable, a new nullable candidate_id FK is added, and a CHECK requires at least one linkage target; source_id and snapshot_id stay NOT NULL). app/ingest/scan.py adds run_scan(source, fetcher, session) that runs fetch -> canonicalize -> extract -> validate and persists a hashed Snapshot, Candidate rows in a pre-publication state, Evidence for OFFICIAL sources only, DiscoveryCandidate quarantine rows for COMMUNITY sources only, and updates ScanRun counts + Source.health. Content hashing is deterministic so re-scanning identical input yields identical candidate hashes, changes_count=0, and zero ChangeEvent rows.
scope: migrations/versions/0004_ingest_candidates.py: create `candidate` and `discovery_candidate`; ALTER `evidence` (add candidate_id FK, make offer_version_id nullable, add CHECK ck_evidence_evidence_link_target). Reversible upgrade/downgrade.
apps/api/app/models/vocab.py: add VERIFICATION_STATES (mirrors app.ingest.vocab), IMPORT_METHODS, DISCOVERY_VERIFICATION_STATUSES.
apps/api/app/models/domain.py: add Candidate + DiscoveryCandidate ORM models; update Evidence (nullable offer_version_id, candidate_id FK, CHECK). Export via app/models/__init__.py.

apps/api/app/ingest/scan.py: run_scan(source, fetcher, session) orchestration + a small adapter registry (build_adapter) keyed by Source.adapter_type (reference JSON adapter for this slice). Deterministic content_hash + candidate_key and change detection.
tests/integration/test_ingest_scan.py (live Postgres) and updates to tests/unit/test_domain_models.py + a vocab drift-guard test.
scripts/stack-smoke.ps1 + scripts/stack-smoke.sh: assert the 0004 tables exist.
docs/DATA_MODEL.md: document the candidate/discovery_candidate tables and the evidence candidate-stage linkage.
out_of_scope: Creating or mutating offer/offer_version (no publication path). The offer_version immutability trigger is untouched.
Real provider adapters, live network scanning, MCP/RSS/HTML transports (Slice 1 fetch seam is reused as-is; no second network path).
Change-event/offer-history writes, conflict review workflows, admin UI, API routes.
Residual R1 (DNS-rebinding IP pinning) from Slice 1 — explicitly deferred.
Any new runtime or npm dependency (requirements-sync guard stays green). Marking F004 passes:true. Advancing to Slice 3.
acceptance_criteria: A FixtureFetcher-driven scan of an OFFICIAL source produces a ScanRun with correct documents/candidates/changes/errors counts, a hashed Snapshot, Candidate rows in a pre-publication state, and Evidence rows linked to the Source + Snapshot (+ Candidate).
Re-scanning identical input yields identical candidate content hashes, changes_count == 0 on the second run, and ZERO ChangeEvent rows (idempotent/reproducible).
run_scan NEVER creates or mutates offer or offer_version rows, and the offer_version immutability trigger remains installed and intact.
A COMMUNITY-source scan creates DiscoveryCandidate quarantine rows and unverified Candidate rows and creates NO Evidence and NO offer_version rows.
Only sources with trust_level == 'official' create evidence rows; community sources never write evidence.
Migration 0004 is reversible: upgrade -> downgrade -> upgrade leaves no model/migration drift (compare_metadata == []), and the 0001/0002/0003 tables + immutability trigger survive intact.
stack-smoke asserts the candidate and discovery_candidate tables exist on the running stack.

Regression green: scripts/test.ps1 and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit pass offline (ruff, format, pytest, prettier, eslint, detect-secrets, pip-audit, npm audit) including the requirements-sync guard (no new dependency).
A fresh-context independent Level 2 evaluator records an adversarial verdict for Slice 2 before any status change; F004 remains passes:false.

risks.data_migration: 0004 creates two tables and alters evidence (add nullable candidate_id FK, relax offer_version_id to nullable, add CHECK). Reversible. offer_version and its immutability trigger are untouched. Making offer_version_id nullable does NOT weaken published-evidence provenance: source_id + snapshot_id remain NOT NULL, FKs still enforced when present, and the new CHECK requires a linkage target.
risks.security_privacy: Community prose is quarantined in discovery_candidate and never linked to evidence/offer_version; official-only evidence rule enforced in run_scan and tested. No credentials handled; detect-secrets stays green.
risks.portability: Pure standard-library Python + SQLAlchemy/Alembic already in use; no new dependency; amd64/arm64 unaffected.
risks.zero_cost_safety: No offer data is published; candidates are pre-publication and never verified; no Z0/free claim is asserted.
risks.regression: No dependency change (requirements-sync green). Existing evidence provenance tests still pass (FKs enforced; dangling FK still rejected). One unit test updated to reflect the intentional evidence nullability/linkage change.
risks.supply_chain: No new Python or npm dependency.

notes: Owner approved the F004 plan and directed Slice 2 implementation, then stop for a fresh-context Level 2 slice evaluation before Slice 3.
